name: Carbonne: Coteaux and Riverside Trail
name_original: Carbonne – boucle trail les Coteaux et Via Garona
description: Jog the Carbonne – trail loop, a moderate 5.8-mile route combining Coteaux paths and Via Garona, with Pyrenees views.
region_intro: Jogging around Carbonne offers diverse terrain in the Haute-Garonne department of southwestern France. The region is characterized by its location at the confluence of the Garonne and Arize rivers, providing numerous riverside paths. Undulating hillsides, known as Coteaux, offer varied elevation and distant views, including glimpses of the Pyrenees mountains. This combination of waterways and rolling landscapes creates a varied environment for running routes. Best jogging routes around Carbonne The most popular jogging route is Carbonne: Coteaux and Riverside Trail, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 8 minutes to complete. This route combines hillside paths with scenic riverside sections. Another top favourite among local runners is Trail along the Garonne loop from Carbonne, a difficult 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This route follows the Garonne river, offering extensive scenic paths along the waterway. Local runners also love the Lit de l'Arize loop from Carbonne, a 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail leading through the Arize river valley, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Jogging around Carbonne is defined by riverside paths, undulating hillsides, and distant mountain views. The network of routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult runs, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Carbonne are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Carbonne's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Carbonne?
A: There are over 35 running routes in the Carbonne area, offering a wide variety of options for joggers of all levels. These include easy, moderate, and more challenging trails.

Q: Are there running trails for all skill levels in Carbonne?
A: Yes, Carbonne offers a good range of running trails for various abilities. You'll find 6 easy routes, 23 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for everyone from beginners to experienced runners.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Carbonne area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the routes around Carbonne are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10533472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Garonne loop from Carbonne&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6982212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lit de l'Arize loop from Carbonne&lt;/a&gt;, which offer scenic loops along the rivers.

Q: What kind of scenery or landmarks can I expect on Carbonne's jogging paths?
A: Jogging in Carbonne offers diverse scenery, from picturesque riverside paths along the Garonne and Arize rivers to undulating hillsides (Coteaux) that provide stunning panoramic views, sometimes even glimpses of the Pyrenees mountains. You might also encounter charming villages and historical sites in the wider Volvestre region. For specific points of interest, consider exploring highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1404557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rieux-Volvestre&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3739798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferry on the Garonne André Blancon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there specific running routes along the rivers in Carbonne?
A: Yes, the Garonne and Arize rivers are central to Carbonne's running landscape. You can enjoy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10533472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Garonne loop from Carbonne&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Garonne, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6982212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lit de l'Arize loop from Carbonne&lt;/a&gt;, taking you through the Arize river valley. These offer refreshing environments with the sounds and sights of the waterways.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Carbonne?
A: The running routes in Carbonne are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain that combines riverside paths with challenging hillsides, and the beautiful natural setting that includes distant mountain views.

Q: Are there any routes that combine hillsides and riverside sections?
A: Yes, for a varied experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974179278/carbonne-coteaux-and-riverside-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbonne: Coteaux and Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This moderate 5.8-mile route perfectly blends the challenge of hillside paths with the tranquility of riverside sections, offering both elevation and scenic views.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for advanced runners near Carbonne?
A: For advanced runners seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10533472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Garonne loop from Carbonne&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.2-mile path that follows the Garonne river, providing extensive scenic sections and a good workout. The wider Via Garona (GR861) also offers an extensive network for long-distance excursions.

Q: Can I find a shorter, easier running loop in Carbonne?
A: Yes, for a shorter and more accessible option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6982242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Carbonne&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 3.7-mile route is a great choice for a quicker run without significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Carbonne?
A: Carbonne's diverse landscape makes it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but riverside trails provide some shade. Winter runs are also possible, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are there opportunities for trail running in wooded areas near Carbonne?
A: While Carbonne itself is known for its rivers and hillsides, the nearby Forêt de Bouconne offers additional opportunities for trail running in a wooded environment. This forest provides shade and a different natural setting for those looking for a change of scenery.

Q: Are there any running routes that start from Carbonne itself?
A: Yes, many routes conveniently start directly from Carbonne. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974179278/carbonne-coteaux-and-riverside-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbonne: Coteaux and Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6982242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Carbonne&lt;/a&gt; both begin in the town, making them easily accessible.
tips: Path in the undergrowth on the banks of the Garonne.
The climb from the banks of the Garonne towards Madale offers lovely views of Carbonne.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: This jogging loop, covering approximately 9.36 kilometers, typically takes around 1 hour and 8 minutes to complete at a steady pace.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, featuring accessible paths, paved sections, and natural surfaces. Notably, the 'Balade des Coteaux' portion includes steep climbs with earth and wooden steps, and even ropes for assistance. You'll also encounter paths along the Garonne river, a ford, and wooded areas. Some sections can be slippery when wet.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers superb panoramic views of the Pyrenees mountains and allows for the discovery of the wild environment of the Garonne river. You'll also find Trail along the Garonne and Scenic views of the Garonne and Carbonne. The 'Balade des Coteaux' provides beautiful vistas of Carbonne from elevated points, and there are sections with 'Land Art' and historic sites.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The 'Carbonne – trail loop the Coteaux and Via Garona' is rated as moderate and requires good fitness due to its diverse terrain and elevation changes. The 'Balade des Coteaux' section is described as a 'sporty course with delicate passages' and can be steep, making it less suitable for beginners, especially after rain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: While enjoyable in many seasons, it's important to consider weather conditions. The steep sections can become particularly slippery and difficult in rainy or wintery weather. For the best experience, consider visiting during drier periods to avoid challenging conditions on the 'Coteaux' and 'Via Garona' descents.

faqs.question: Is the Carbonne – trail loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses areas rich in biodiversity, including parts of the Natura 2000 network. While dogs are generally allowed on many trails, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Be mindful of the steep and potentially slippery sections, which might be challenging for some dogs.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a loop, so you can likely find parking in Carbonne itself. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but look for public parking within the town, especially near the start of the 'Coteaux' or 'Via Garona' sections, to begin your jog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the 'Carbonne – trail loop the Coteaux and Via Garona'. It is open for public use.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I see?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses an area rich in natural heritage, including a Natura 2000 network zone. You might spot various trees, shrubs, wildflowers (such as wild orchids), and wildlife like hares, partridges, and deer. The wild environment of the Garonne river also contributes to diverse flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop ingeniously combines local paths with segments of the historic Via Garona (GR 861). It also passes through sections of the Balade au fil de l’eau and the Balade des coteaux, among others.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for jogging this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the diverse and sometimes challenging terrain, appropriate jogging shoes with good grip are essential. For the steeper sections, especially after rain, walking sticks are highly recommended. Carrying water is also advisable, as specific water points on the trail are not mentioned.

waypoints.name: Scenic views of the Garonne and Carbonne
